ItacPersonItem.Notes: Joined the Ricovrati in 1689. On the 16th of June 1691, there was a public meeting which was attended by several women, including Lucrezia Capello Gradenigo, the spouse of the `podesta` `, Maria Zorzi Loredana, and several other women from Padua and from aborad. In this context, Obizzi delivered the following oration: `Se fusse piu` lodevole il costume d`escluder le donne dal governo come i Romani, o pure d`ammetterle come i Germani`. Apparently, the topic raised a heated debate. Marco Andrea Zambelli agreed with the first point, while Matteo Forzadura stood for the second option. A couple of years later, in the public meeting which took place in Mantova Benavides family garden, Obizzi had a conversation with Vitaliano Mussato on the following problem: se porti piu` vantaggio nel cuor delle donne a chi le serve il sofferir le loro amorose ingiurie o il rissentirsene.
ItacPersonItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
